roles.346422: triggering alert: a right-to-left-override screen-saver executable runs from ProgramData
roles.346533: the masqueraded process initiates an outbound TCP connection
roles.346599: the masqueraded process also spawns PowerShell

metadata.security_proposition: The document-looking screensaver execution belongs to an active malicious payload chain on the host.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG. The masqueraded screen-saver executable runs, opens an outbound connection, and spawns command shells. The benign-looking filename must not cause under-triage. Do not leak to model.

curation_notes: Subtle-strong (up-rank): surface looks like a user opening a document (explorer -> rcs.3aka3.doc), but the filename carries a U+202E RTLO override and is really cod.3aka3.scr, a screensaver executable launched with /S.

Confirming evidence (quiet, needs inspection): an internal C2 beacon to 192.168.0.5:1234 and a cmd->powershell payload chain. Correct verdict malicious/high despite the document-like surface. (The sdclt UAC-bypass from the same chain is carved into UAC-001.)

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
